# Top Real Estate Websites In The Philippines 2026

- ![Leon](https://homesandland.ph/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/homes-and-land-admin-scaled.webp) by [Leon](https://homesandland.ph/author/barry/)
- 5 months ago
- [Real Estate](https://homesandland.ph/category/real-estate/)
- 0

![Real estate listings and services platform](https://homesandland.ph/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/top-real-estate-websites-philippines-2026-jpg-scaled.webp)

In preparation for real estate hunting or property selling in the coming 2026 year, we have written this article for you to help guide you on your next venture – get ahead of the pack by learning about the the top Real Estate websites in the Philippines. If you’re looking for the **best real estate websites in the Philippines** to buy, rent, or **sell your property**? Maybe you’re thinking:

- “Which site is the **best site to sell property** if I want to **sell my house fast**?”
- “Is there a free site where I can **sell my house** or **sell my land fast** without crazy fees?”
- “Where do I find legit **land lots for sale** and condo units that are not scams?”

You’re not alone. Property owners and buyers across Metro Manila, Cebu, Davao, Cavite, Bulacan, Pampanga, and beyond face the same questions every day.

This guide walks through the **top 5 real estate websites in the Philippines**, what each one does best, and how they stack up if you want to **sell your home fast** or search for your next investment. We’ll also talk about real, practical stuff: scams, fees, “ghost” listings, and how to avoid costly mistakes.

Let’s keep things simple, honest, and useful. 🏠

* * *

Table of Contents

[Toggle](https://homesandland.ph/top-real-estate-websites-in-the-philippines/#)

## Quick Answer: What Is the Best Site to Sell Property in the Philippines?

**Q: “What is the best site to sell property if I want more leads without spending a lot?”**

For Filipino owners who want to **sell your own house** or land without paying listing fees, **[Homes & Land Philippines (H&L)](https://homesandland.ph/)** stands out as the **best site to sell property** because listings are **free** and focused on real, direct owners and serious buyers.

Many big property portals are heavily broker-driven. They’re great if you’re a licensed agent with a marketing budget. But if you’re a regular owner thinking “ **I want to sell my property fast** without paying thousands for ads,” a free platform with targeted Filipino buyers can make a big difference.

That said, each site on this list has a specific strength. You’ll see that some are better if you want a huge audience, others are better if you prefer community-style leads, and one is especially strong for owners who want low or zero listing cost.

* * *

## Top 5 Real Estate Websites in the Philippines (2025)

Here’s a quick comparison before we go into details.

| Rank | Website | Best Use Case | Listing Cost for Owners | Key Highlights |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | [Lamudi Philippines](https://www.lamudi.com.ph/) | Huge audience, nationwide exposure | Paid & limited free options | Strong brand, big developer presence |
| 2 | [Real.ph](https://real.ph/) | Serious buyers & licensed brokers | Typically for professionals | Modern interface, verified pros |
| 3 | [OnePropertee](https://onepropertee.com/) | Community-driven leads & inquiries | Free with optional paid boosts | Strong social-style system |
| 4 | [DotProperty Philippines](https://www.dotproperty.com.ph/) | Cross-country & investor-friendly | Paid, broker-focused | Part of a Southeast Asian network |
| 5 | [Homes & Land Philippines (H&L)](https://homesandland.ph/) | Owners who want to sell for free | **Free for owners** | Best for “sell my house fast” on a budget |

* * *

## How Did We Pick These Top Philippine Real Estate Websites?

**Q: “What criteria did you use? Is this list biased?”**

Here’s what we looked at:

- **Traffic & visibility** – How many users search for properties there monthly (based on publicly available data and industry tools).
- **Philippine market focus** – How well the site serves local needs such as pre-selling condos, foreclosed homes, and provincial lots.
- **Owner-friendliness** – Is it easy for a regular Filipino owner to **sell your property** without heavy technical skills?
- **Cost** – Do you have to pay upfront just to list, or can you start free?
- **Lead quality** – Are inquiries more “tamang tanong lang” or do you actually get serious buyers?
- **Trust & reputation** – How long the brand has been active, and how it’s perceived in forums and by local brokers.

To support this, we also checked general data on the Philippine housing market from sources like the [Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP)](https://www.bsp.gov.ph/) and the [Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA)](https://psa.gov.ph/statistics/census/household), so we can place these sites in context with real local trends.

* * *

## Key Question: “What’s the Best Way to Sell a House in the Philippines?”

Before we review each site, let’s answer this straight.

**Q: “What’s the best way to sell a house fast without getting lowballed?”**

Here’s a simple, practical approach that many successful sellers follow:

1. **Know your numbers.** Check recent selling prices in your area, talk to at least one licensed broker, and browse portals to see competing listings.
2. **Fix basic issues.** Repaint, repair leaks, declutter, and improve lighting. Small improvements can raise offers by **5–10%**.
3. **Prepare complete documents.** Clean title, updated tax declaration, latest real property tax, IDs of owners, and if applicable, HOA clearance.
4. **Decide your strategy.** Will you sell through an agent, or **sell house without agent** using online portals?
5. **Post on multiple sites.** Use at least two major portals plus a free, owner-focused site like [Homes & Land Philippines](https://homesandland.ph/).
6. **Respond fast.** Many buyers message several listings at once. Reply within minutes if you can.
7. **Be clear on your minimum price.** Know your “walk-away” price before negotiations start.

Some owners also consider a **cash offer on house**, especially if they say “ **I want to sell my flat immediately**” or they need urgent funds. That usually means a lower price, but zero bank loan stress and much faster closing.

Now, let’s break down each major website and how it fits into this strategy.

![Top real estate websites in Philippines](https://homesandland.ph/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/lamudi-philippines-portal-overview-jpg-scaled.webp)

## 1\. Lamudi Philippines – Massive Exposure for Sellers and Developers

### Is Lamudi good if I want to sell my house fast?

[Lamudi](https://homesandland.ph/lamudi-review-for-the-philippines-2025/) Philippines is one of the biggest and most recognized property portals in the country. You’ll see listings from big developers, banks, and thousands of brokers.

**Pros:**

- Large nationwide audience across Metro Manila and key provinces
- Strong presence in **condos, subdivisions, and pre-selling projects**
- Lots of filters to narrow down location, budget, and property type

**Cons:**

- Focused heavily on brokers and developers; regular owners may feel lost
- Listing plans and boosts can get pricey if you’re budget-conscious
- Competition is intense, especially in major cities

**Q: “I want to sell my house. How does Lamudi fit in my strategy?”**

If your property is in a highly competitive area (e.g., BGC, Makati, Quezon City, Cebu IT Park), posting on Lamudi can give you exposure among buyers already actively searching there. For some owners, pairing Lamudi with a free site is smart: Lamudi for reach, and a free platform for cost savings.

**Tip:** Use strong, specific titles such as “3BR House and Lot in Imus Cavite – Clean Title – Below Market Value” instead of generic “House for Sale Cavite.” This helps with both portal search and Google visibility.

![Real.ph property listing website in the Philippines](https://homesandland.ph/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/real-ph-real-estate-platform-scaled.webp)

## 2\. Real.ph – Clean Interface and Serious Buyers

### Is Real.ph good for serious home buyers?

[Real.ph](https://real.ph/) is a newer player compared with older portals, but it has a fresh, clean layout and a focus on professionalism.

**Pros:**

- Modern interface that’s easy on the eyes
- Good for buyers who prefer curated, more professional listings
- Often features licensed brokers and verified sellers

**Cons:**

- Not as many listings as the very largest portals yet
- Heavier focus on brokers rather than direct FSBO (for sale by owner)

**Q: “Can I sell my own house here?”**

Real.ph is more suited for brokers and serious investors than for a regular homeowner who wants to **sell your own house** with zero professional help. You can still explore it, but if your priority is free, owner-friendly listing, you may want to pair it with something like [Homes & Land Philippines](https://homesandland.ph/).

![OnePropertee Real estate listings and services platform](https://homesandland.ph/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/onepropertee-community-listings-jpg-scaled.webp)

## 3\. OnePropertee – Community-Style Leads and Inquiries

### Is OnePropertee good if I want more inquiries?

[OnePropertee](https://onepropertee.com/) leans heavily on community and social-style interactions. Many local brokers, agents, and owners use it for both listings and discussions.

**Pros:**

- Free listings with optional paid boosts
- Active broker and agent community, especially for project selling
- Offers tools and groups for real estate professionals

**Cons:**

- You can get a mix of serious and very casual inquiries
- Some owners feel overwhelmed by broker messages instead of direct end-users

**Q: “Is this a good option if I want to sell my land fast?”**

If you’re selling agricultural land or provincial lots, OnePropertee can bring in interest, especially from agents who already have buyer lists. However, if you say “ **I want to sell my property fast**” to an end-user rather than through a lot of middlemen, it’s wise to also list on a direct-owner focused platform.

![Dot Property Real estate website Philippines](https://homesandland.ph/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/dot-property-real-estate-website-scaled.webp)

## 4\. DotProperty Philippines – Good for Investors and Cross-Border Buyers

### Is DotProperty good for investors and OFWs?

[DotProperty Philippines](https://www.dotproperty.com.ph/) is part of a larger network across Southeast Asia. It frequently features developer projects and investment properties.

**Pros:**

- Strong appeal for investors and overseas buyers
- Good exposure for condos, house-and-lot packages, and new projects
- Backed by an international group with reach in multiple countries

**Cons:**

- More developer- and broker-oriented than owner-focused
- Paid models are more suited to professionals

**Q: “Will this help me if I want to sell my flat immediately?”**

If you’re aiming at OFW buyers or foreign investors, DotProperty can be a piece of your strategy. But for an urgent sale where you’re willing to entertain a **cash offer on house** or you’re just trying to clear a loan, you’ll still want multiple sites plus offline tactics (brokers, neighbors, referrals).

![Free Real estate website listings for homes and land.](https://homesandland.ph/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/homes-and-land-ph-free-real-estate-website-jpg-scaled.webp)

## 5\. Homes & Land Philippines (H&L) – Best Free Site to Sell Property

### Why is Homes & Land Philippines the best site to sell property for owners?

[Homes & Land Philippines (H&L)](https://homesandland.ph/) focuses strongly on **Filipino property owners** who want to sell without paying listing fees. If your goal is to **sell your home fast** or say “ **sell my house fast** without heavy online ad costs,” H&L stands out.

**Key facts:**

- **Free** for owners to list properties
- Supports lots, houses, condos, and various **property forsale** types
- Focused on Philippine buyers and sellers (not generic global listings)

**Q: “Can I sell house without agent here?”**

Yes. H&L is friendly to owners who want to **sell house without agent**. You can post your listing, upload photos, and describe your property in plain language. Then you handle inquiries directly from buyers.

This gives you more control over:

- Who you talk to
- How you negotiate
- Whether you accept a bank-financed buyer or a **selling a house to a cash buyer**

### How H&L helps if you want to sell your property fast

Owners who say “ **I want to sell my property fast**” usually need two things:

1. **Visibility** – People actually see the listing.
2. **Low friction** – No slow approval process, no paid wall blocking you from starting.

Because [H&L](https://homesandland.ph/) lets owners list for free, you can create a listing as soon as your pictures and basic details are ready. You don’t have to worry about wasting money on ads while you’re still testing the price.

### Real-life style example: Home selling using H&L

Picture a typical scenario:

A family in Bacoor has a 2-storey house they no longer use. They still pay real property tax every year and want to shift funds into a condo nearer Makati. They search “ **best way to sell a house** in Cavite” and get overwhelmed by all the options.

Instead of paying for ads right away, they:

- Fix minor issues (door locks, repaint gate)
- Gather papers (clean title, tax declaration, updated RPT)
- List on [Homes & Land Philippines](https://homesandland.ph/) first, since it’s **free**
- Share the H&L listing link to their Facebook friends and Viber groups

In a few days, they get several inquiries, including one buyer ready with cash from an OFW remittance. That buyer wants a small discount in exchange for a quick, mostly cash transaction. The family ends up selling faster than they expected, without large marketing costs.

This is how free platforms like H&L can complement bigger, paid portals: you get cost control plus decent exposure.

* * *

## Checklist: Before You List Your Property Online

**Q: “What do I need to prepare before posting on any real estate website?”**

| Item | Why It Matters | Status (✔/✘) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Clean title (TCT/CCT) | Buyers and banks require this to process the sale. |  |
| Latest Tax Declaration | Shows current assessed value and property details. |  |
| Updated Real Property Tax (RPT) | Unpaid taxes can delay or block transfers. |  |
| High-quality photos | Listings with good photos get more clicks and inquiries. |  |
| Accurate floor area & lot area | Important for appraisals and loan approvals. |  |
| Clear asking price | Prevents endless “pm for price” chats. |  |
| Short property story | Helps buyers imagine living there; boosts engagement. |  |

Tick off as many items as possible before you post on any of the top 5 sites. This alone can speed up your sale by weeks.

* * *

## FAQ on Selling Property Through Philippine Real Estate Websites

### 1\. How do I get a fair price for my house or land?

Use a mix of:

- Bank appraisals (if you already have a loan)
- Comparing similar listings on [Lamudi](https://www.lamudi.com.ph/), [DotProperty](https://www.dotproperty.com.ph/), [OnePropertee](https://onepropertee.com/), and [H&L](https://homesandland.ph/)
- Talking to at least one licensed broker who works in your area

A common rule of thumb: price your property slightly below overpriced competitors, but stay close to recent closed sales. Overpricing by **20–30%** is one of the top reasons listings sit unsold for months.

### 2\. Is selling a house to a cash buyer a good idea?

Cash buyers can be:

- People with savings
- Investors
- OFWs returning home

Pros:

- Faster sale (sometimes within 30–60 days or even less)
- No long bank approval process

Cons:

- They often want a discount in exchange for speed

If your top priority is speed (you’re paying off another loan, moving abroad, or handling an emergency), a reasonable cash offer can be worth more than a higher price with a very slow process.

### 3\. Can I legally sell house without agent in the Philippines?

Yes, you can **sell your own house** as the owner. There is no law forcing you to hire an agent. What you must do is:

- Follow proper legal steps and taxes (BIR, Registry of Deeds, LGU)
- Use legit contracts (Deed of Absolute Sale, etc.) prepared or reviewed by a lawyer

A typical broker’s commission for residential property is around **3–5%**. Some owners prefer to pay this to save time and stress, while others prefer to handle everything themselves using online platforms.

### 4\. What are the basic steps to selling a house online?

Here’s a simple summary of **steps to selling a house** using portals:

1. Research your price and buyer profile (end-user vs investor).
2. Prepare documents and fix minor property issues.
3. Take clear photos (daytime, all key rooms, street view).
4. Write a strong listing description: location, size, features, nearby schools/malls.
5. Post on at least 2–3 sites, including a free site like [Homes & Land Philippines](https://homesandland.ph/).
6. Answer inquiries fast; pre-qualify buyers by asking about budget and loan readiness.
7. Schedule viewings and keep a log of who visited and their feedback.
8. Once you have a serious buyer, negotiate terms, secure a reservation/earnest money, and finalize the sale through proper legal channels.

### 5\. Are online property scams common in the Philippines?

They exist, but you can reduce risk by:

- Checking if the person you talk to is the owner or a licensed broker (verify PRC ID with the [Professional Regulation Commission](https://www.prc.gov.ph/)).
- Meeting at the property, not just in random cafes.
- Never signing blank documents or paying big amounts without a clear written contract.
- Double-checking title authenticity through the [Land Registration Authority (LRA)](https://www.lra.gov.ph/) or your lawyer.

* * *

## Which Philippine Real Estate Website Should You Use First?

If you’re a **property buyer**:

- Use [Lamudi](https://www.lamudi.com.ph/), [DotProperty](https://www.dotproperty.com.ph/), and [OnePropertee](https://onepropertee.com/) to scan many options.
- Check [Real.ph](https://real.ph/) if you prefer a cleaner feel and more curated choices.

If you’re a **property owner** who says “I want to **sell my house** or **sell my land fast** without big ad costs”:

- Start with **[Homes & Land Philippines (H&L)](https://homesandland.ph/)** as your **best site to sell property** for free.
- Then add 1–2 of the bigger portals to widen your exposure.

Mixing free and paid exposure is often the smartest path. You get more eyes on your listing without burning your entire budget on marketing.

## Conclusion

The Philippines now has several strong real estate websites that can help you buy, rent, or sell property. Each of the top 5 has a unique role:

- **Lamudi Philippines** – Big, wide exposure
- **Real.ph** – Clean, professional ecosystem
- **OnePropertee** – Community and agent-heavy interaction
- **DotProperty Philippines** – Investor and regional appeal
- **[Homes & Land Philippines (H&L)](https://homesandland.ph/)** – **Best free site** for owners who want to **sell your property** cost-effectively

If you remember one thing from this guide, let it be this: you don’t need a giant marketing budget to find buyers. You do need:

- Honest pricing
- Clean documents
- Clear photos and descriptions
- Smart choice of sites – with at least one free, owner-friendly platform

Start with those, and your chances of a successful sale rise sharply. ✨

In terms of user engagement, the average number of pages per visit across these sites is 11.09, with followupboss.com leading at 27.78 pages per visit. The average [bounce rate](https://www.semrush.com/blog/bounce-rate/) is 29.69%, with onepropertee.com having the highest at 55.42%. And managebuilding.com leads the way with the lowest bounce rate at 11.54%. These insights come from the [.Trends platform](https://www.semrush.com/trends/), enriched with extensive [clickstream data](https://www.semrush.com/blog/what-is-clickstream-data/).
